page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture using Neoverse-V2 cores, this server features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is supported by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balance of average single-core capabilities and weaker multi-core scaling due to the dual-core configuration. The instance achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1707.1 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1815.0, both placing in the average tier. However, multi-core metrics like the Geekbench multi-core score of 3223.0 and the stress-ng multi-core score of 3402.08 are categorized as weak. Memory performance is mixed, with a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.16 GB/sec and an average large-block read bandwidth of 56.37 GB/sec, contrasted by weaker write speeds and small-block read bandwidths.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.large offers a specialized hardware profile that pairs ARM-based energy efficiency with high memory density and fast local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff lies in its limited parallel processing power, as evidenced by poor performance in extended instruction tests and multi-threaded workloads. Consequently, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications with low parallel compute requirements, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1815.0 and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression of 14.16 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory write speeds are relatively weak. This instance offers a cost-effective, high-memory profile with fast local storage, making it suitable for memory-sensitive workloads with modest parallel compute needs, such as database operations, caching, and single-threaded data processing.
